# Proverbs 25

## Verses (28)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | These are more wise sayings of Solomon, copied out by the men of Hezekiah, king of Judah. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | It is the glory of God to keep a thing secret: but the glory of kings is to have it searched out. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | The heaven is high and the earth is deep, and the hearts of kings may not be searched out. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Take away the waste from silver, and a vessel will come out for the silver-worker. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Take away evil-doers from before the king, and the seat of his power will be made strong in righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Do not take glory for yourself before the king, and do not put yourself in the place of the great: | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | For it is better to have it said to you, Come up here; than for you to be put down in a lower place before the ruler. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Do not be quick to go to law about what you have seen, for what will you do in the end, when your neighbour has put you to shame? | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Have a talk with your neighbour himself about your cause, but do not give away the secret of another: | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Or your hearer may say evil of you, and your shame will not be turned away. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | A word at the right time is like apples of gold in a network of silver. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Like a nose-ring of gold and an ornament of the best gold, is a wise man who says sharp words to an ear ready to give attention. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | As the cold of snow in the time of grain-cutting, so is a true servant to those who send him; for he gives new life to the soul of his master. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | As clouds and wind without rain, so is one who takes credit for an offering he has not given. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | A judge is moved by one who for a long time undergoes wrongs without protest, and by a soft tongue even bone is broken. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | If you have honey, take only as much as is enough for you; for fear that, being full of it, you may not be able to keep it down. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Let not your foot be frequently in your neighbour's house, or he may get tired of you, and his feeling be turned to hate. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | One who gives false witness against his neighbour is a hammer and a sword and a sharp arrow. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Putting one's faith in a false man in time of trouble is like a broken tooth and a shaking foot. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Like one who takes off clothing in cold weather and like acid on a wound, is he who makes melody to a sad heart. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | If your hater is in need of food, give him bread; and if he is in need of drink, give him water: | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | For so you will put coals of fire on his head, and the Lord will give you your reward. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | As the north wind gives birth to rain, so is an angry face caused by a tongue saying evil secretly. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | It is better to be living in an angle of the house-top, than with a bitter-tongued woman in a wide house. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | As cold water to a tired soul, so is good news from a far country. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | Like a troubled fountain and a dirty spring, is an upright man who has to give way before evil-doers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | It is not good to take much honey: so he who is not looking for honour will be honoured. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | He whose spirit is uncontrolled is like an unwalled town which has been broken into. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |

<!-- END AUTO-INDEX -->
